title: What Money Actually Is
body:
Money is a coordination tool. It can act as a medium of exchange, a unit of account, and a store of value, which is why it is so central to modern life.

For individuals, money is not just currency. It is the rhythm of income and expenses, the stress of bills, the relief of savings, and the freedom that comes from having options.

A good money system does not need to make people obsessed with wealth. It needs to make daily life legible and keep the future from becoming an emergency.
keyPoints: Money coordinates people who do not know each other
Cashflow is the practical centre of personal finance
Inflation changes the real meaning of savings
Debt is a tool that can help or harm
Financial literacy is a life skill, not a niche interest
furtherReading: The Psychology of Money by Morgan Housel
I Will Teach You to Be Rich by Ramit Sethi
The Barefoot Investor by Scott Pape
